speaker: KING
text: And can you by no drift of conference

speaker: KING
text: Get from him why he puts on this confusion,

speaker: KING
text: Grating so harshly all his days of quiet

speaker: KING
text: With turbulent and dangerous lunacy?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: He does confess he feels himself distracted,

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: But from what cause he will by no means speak.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Nor do we find him forward to be sounded,

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: But with a crafty madness keeps aloof

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: When we would bring him on to some confession

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Of his true state.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Did he receive you well?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Most like a gentleman.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: But with much forcing of his disposition.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Niggard of question, but of our demands

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Most free in his reply.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Did you assay him to any pastime?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Madam, it so fell out that certain players

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: We o’erraught on the way. Of these we told him,

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: And there did seem in him a kind of joy

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To hear of it. They are here about the court,

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: And, as I think, they have already order

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: This night to play before him.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: ’Tis most true,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And he beseeched me to entreat your Majesties

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To hear and see the matter.

speaker: KING
text: With all my heart, and it doth much content me

speaker: KING
text: To hear him so inclined.

speaker: KING
text: Good gentlemen, give him a further edge

speaker: KING
text: And drive his purpose into these delights.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: We shall, my lord.

speaker: KING
text: Sweet Gertrude, leave us too,

speaker: KING
text: For we have closely sent for Hamlet hither,

speaker: KING
text: That he, as ’twere by accident, may here

speaker: KING
text: Affront Ophelia.

speaker: KING
text: Her father and myself, lawful espials,

speaker: KING
text: Will so bestow ourselves that, seeing unseen,

speaker: KING
text: We may of their encounter frankly judge

speaker: KING
text: And gather by him, as he is behaved,

speaker: KING
text: If ’t be th’ affliction of his love or no

speaker: KING
text: That thus he suffers for.

speaker: QUEEN
text: I shall obey you.

speaker: QUEEN
text: And for your part, Ophelia, I do wish

speaker: QUEEN
text: That your good beauties be the happy cause

speaker: QUEEN
text: Of Hamlet’s wildness. So shall I hope your virtues

speaker: QUEEN
text: Will bring him to his wonted way again,

speaker: QUEEN
text: To both your honors.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Madam, I wish it may.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Ophelia, walk you here.—Gracious, so please you,

speaker: POLONIUS
text:
We will bestow ourselves.
                 
                To
                 
                Ophelia
                .
               Read on this book,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That show of such an exercise may color

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Your loneliness.—We are oft to blame in this

speaker: POLONIUS
text: (’Tis too much proved), that with devotion’s visage

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And pious action we do sugar o’er

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The devil himself.

speaker: KING
text: O, ’tis too true!

speaker: KING
text: How smart a lash that speech doth give my conscience.

speaker: KING
text: The harlot’s cheek beautied with plast’ring art

speaker: KING
text: Is not more ugly to the thing that helps it

speaker: KING
text: Than is my deed to my most painted word.

speaker: KING
text: O heavy burden!

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I hear him coming. Let’s withdraw, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: To be or not to be—that is the question:

speaker: HAMLET
text: Whether ’tis nobler in the mind to suffer

speaker: HAMLET
text: The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or to take arms against a sea of troubles

speaker: HAMLET
text: And, by opposing, end them. To die, to sleep—

speaker: HAMLET
text: No more—and by a sleep to say we end

speaker: HAMLET
text: The heartache and the thousand natural shocks

speaker: HAMLET
text: That flesh is heir to—’tis a consummation

speaker: HAMLET
text: Devoutly to be wished. To die, to sleep—

speaker: HAMLET
text: To sleep, perchance to dream. Ay, there’s the rub,

speaker: HAMLET
text: For in that sleep of death what dreams may come,

speaker: HAMLET
text: When we have shuffled off this mortal coil,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Must give us pause. There’s the respect

speaker: HAMLET
text: That makes calamity of so long life.

speaker: HAMLET
text: For who would bear the whips and scorns of time,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Th’ oppressor’s wrong, the proud man’s contumely,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The pangs of despised love, the law’s delay,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The insolence of office, and the spurns

speaker: HAMLET
text: That patient merit of th’ unworthy takes,

speaker: HAMLET
text: When he himself might his quietus make

speaker: HAMLET
text: With a bare bodkin? Who would fardels bear,

speaker: HAMLET
text: To grunt and sweat under a weary life,

speaker: HAMLET
text: But that the dread of something after death,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The undiscovered country from whose bourn

speaker: HAMLET
text: No traveler returns, puzzles the will

speaker: HAMLET
text: And makes us rather bear those ills we have

speaker: HAMLET
text: Than fly to others that we know not of?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thus conscience does make cowards of us all,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And thus the native hue of resolution

speaker: HAMLET
text: Is sicklied o’er with the pale cast of thought,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And enterprises of great pitch and moment

speaker: HAMLET
text: With this regard their currents turn awry

speaker: HAMLET
text: And lose the name of action.—Soft you now,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The fair Ophelia.—Nymph, in thy orisons

speaker: HAMLET
text: Be all my sins remembered.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Good my lord,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: How does your Honor for this many a day?

speaker: HAMLET
text: I humbly thank you, well.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My lord, I have remembrances of yours

speaker: OPHELIA
text: That I have longèd long to redeliver.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: I pray you now receive them.

speaker: HAMLET
text: No, not I. I never gave you aught.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My honored lord, you know right well you did,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And with them words of so sweet breath composed

speaker: OPHELIA
text: As made the things more rich. Their perfume lost,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Take these again, for to the noble mind

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Rich gifts wax poor when givers prove unkind.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: There, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ha, ha, are you honest?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Are you fair?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: What means your Lordship?

speaker: HAMLET
text: That if you be honest and fair, your honesty

speaker: HAMLET
text: should admit no discourse to your beauty.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Could beauty, my lord, have better commerce

speaker: OPHELIA
text: than with honesty?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, truly, for the power of beauty will sooner

speaker: HAMLET
text: transform honesty from what it is to a bawd than

speaker: HAMLET
text: the force of honesty can translate beauty into his

speaker: HAMLET
text: likeness. This was sometime a paradox, but now

speaker: HAMLET
text: the time gives it proof. I did love you once.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Indeed, my lord, you made me believe so.

speaker: HAMLET
text: You should not have believed me, for virtue

speaker: HAMLET
text: cannot so inoculate our old stock but we shall

speaker: HAMLET
text: relish of it. I loved you not.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: I was the more deceived.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Get thee to a nunnery. Why wouldst thou be

speaker: HAMLET
text: a breeder of sinners? I am myself indifferent honest,

speaker: HAMLET
text: but yet I could accuse me of such things that it

speaker: HAMLET
text: were better my mother had not borne me: I am

speaker: HAMLET
text: very proud, revengeful, ambitious, with more offenses

speaker: HAMLET
text: at my beck than I have thoughts to put them

speaker: HAMLET
text: in, imagination to give them shape, or time to act

speaker: HAMLET
text: them in. What should such fellows as I do crawling

speaker: HAMLET
text: between earth and heaven? We are arrant knaves

speaker: HAMLET
text: all; believe none of us. Go thy ways to a nunnery.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Where’s your father?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: At home, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let the doors be shut upon him that he may

speaker: HAMLET
text: play the fool nowhere but in ’s own house. Farewell.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: O, help him, you sweet heavens!

speaker: HAMLET
text: If thou dost marry, I’ll give thee this plague

speaker: HAMLET
text: for thy dowry: be thou as chaste as ice, as pure as

speaker: HAMLET
text: snow, thou shalt not escape calumny. Get thee to a

speaker: HAMLET
text: nunnery, farewell. Or if thou wilt needs marry,

speaker: HAMLET
text: marry a fool, for wise men know well enough what

speaker: HAMLET
text: monsters you make of them. To a nunnery, go, and

speaker: HAMLET
text: quickly too. Farewell.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Heavenly powers, restore him!

speaker: HAMLET
text: I have heard of your paintings too, well

speaker: HAMLET
text: enough. God hath given you one face, and you

speaker: HAMLET
text: make yourselves another. You jig and amble, and

speaker: HAMLET
text: you lisp; you nickname God’s creatures and make

speaker: HAMLET
text: your wantonness your ignorance. Go to, I’ll no

speaker: HAMLET
text: more on ’t. It hath made me mad. I say we will have

speaker: HAMLET
text: no more marriage. Those that are married already,

speaker: HAMLET
text: all but one, shall live. The rest shall keep as they are.

speaker: HAMLET
text: To a nunnery, go.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: O, what a noble mind is here o’erthrown!

speaker: OPHELIA
text: The courtier’s, soldier’s, scholar’s, eye, tongue, sword,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Th’ expectancy and rose of the fair state,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: The glass of fashion and the mold of form,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Th’ observed of all observers, quite, quite down!

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And I, of ladies most deject and wretched,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: That sucked the honey of his musicked vows,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Now see that noble and most sovereign reason,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Like sweet bells jangled, out of time and harsh;

speaker: OPHELIA
text: That unmatched form and stature of blown youth

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Blasted with ecstasy. O, woe is me

speaker: OPHELIA
text: T’ have seen what I have seen, see what I see!

speaker: KING
text: Love? His affections do not that way tend;

speaker: KING
text: Nor what he spake, though it lacked form a little,

speaker: KING
text: Was not like madness. There’s something in his soul

speaker: KING
text: O’er which his melancholy sits on brood,

speaker: KING
text: And I do doubt the hatch and the disclose

speaker: KING
text: Will be some danger; which for to prevent,

speaker: KING
text: I have in quick determination

speaker: KING
text: Thus set it down: he shall with speed to England

speaker: KING
text: For the demand of our neglected tribute.

speaker: KING
text: Haply the seas, and countries different,

speaker: KING
text: With variable objects, shall expel

speaker: KING
text: This something-settled matter in his heart,

speaker: KING
text: Whereon his brains still beating puts him thus

speaker: KING
text: From fashion of himself. What think you on ’t?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: It shall do well. But yet do I believe

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The origin and commencement of his grief

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Sprung from neglected love.—How now, Ophelia?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You need not tell us what Lord Hamlet said;

speaker: POLONIUS
text: We heard it all.—My lord, do as you please,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But, if you hold it fit, after the play

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Let his queen-mother all alone entreat him

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To show his grief. Let her be round with him;

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And I’ll be placed, so please you, in the ear

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Of all their conference. If she find him not,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To England send him, or confine him where

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Your wisdom best shall think.

speaker: KING
text: It shall be so.

speaker: KING
text: Madness in great ones must not unwatched go.
